subject_tmpl: Billing Cycle {DATE} — Summary for Finance Review
body_tmpl: David, attached is the summary for the {DATE} billing cycle. We processed {NUM} accounts this run. Total amount billed comes to {AMOUNT}. Delinquent balance carried forward is {AMOUNT}. Three accounts flagged for supervisor review due to disputed reads — account numbers provided in the attachment. Let me know if you need the raw export or if anything looks off before we close the cycle.
reply_tmpl: Lorena, got it. I'll pull those three disputed accounts into the reconciliation spreadsheet before EOD. Everything else looks consistent with last cycle. Thanks for the quick turnaround.

body_tmpl: Hi David, I'm sending over the proposed shutoff list for the week of {DATE}. There are {NUM} accounts that have passed the {DAYS}-day delinquency threshold. Combined outstanding balance is {AMOUNT}. I've already sent the required notice letters. Per policy I need your sign-off before we schedule the field orders. Please advise if any accounts on this list have active payment plans that should hold the shutoff.
reply_tmpl: Lorena — reviewed. Two accounts have active arrangements through my office; I've marked them in the attached. You're clear to schedule the remaining {NUM} for field orders. Copy me on anything over {AMOUNT} per account before action.

body_tmpl: Lorena, wanted to share a quick snapshot of the Water Fund as of {DATE}. Operating balance stands at {AMOUNT}. That's roughly {DAYS} days of operating reserve. Revenue collections are running {NUM}% ahead of the same period last fiscal year. I'm flagging this for the Mayor's monthly briefing packet. If you have any receivables still outstanding that should be reflected, please get them to me by end of day {DATE}.

Mayor Okonkwo, I'm escalating an account dispute that has been unresolved through our normal process. Account {ACCT}, held by {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}, disputes a charge of {AMOUNT} from the {DATE} billing cycle. The resident claims the read was taken during a period when the property was vacant. I've reviewed the meter history and the consumption figure appears anomalous. I'd recommend we authorize a courtesy adjustment and schedule a re-read. Please advise how you'd like me to proceed.
reply_tmpl: Lorena, agree on the re-read. Go ahead and authorize a courtesy hold on the {AMOUNT} charge pending the meter inspection. Keep me posted on the outcome.

body_tmpl: Frank, we've got an unusually high read on the meter at {ADDRESS} for the current billing period. Consumption came in at {NUM} gallons, which is about three times the property's 12-month average. I've put a note on the account, but before I bill at that level I want to confirm there's no meter issue or known main break nearby. Can your crew take a look when in the area? Account number is {ACCT}.
reply_tmpl: Lorena — I'll send someone over to {ADDRESS} on {DATE}. Will report back same day. If it's a meter malfunction we'll document it for the adjustment.

subject_tmpl: My Water Bill Is Way Too High — Need Help
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm reaching out because I received a water bill for {AMOUNT} this month and I have no idea how that's possible. I live alone at {ADDRESS} and my usual bill is around {AMOUNT}. I have not had any leaks that I know of. I called the water office and was told to email you directly. My account number is {ACCT}. I'm a senior on a fixed income and I cannot afford to pay this without some explanation. Please help me understand what happened.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for contacting our office. I've forwarded your concern to our Water Billing Clerk, who will review account {ACCT} and reach out to you within {DAYS} business days. We take billing accuracy seriously and want to make sure you're treated fairly.

body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, here is the monthly Water Fund briefing. Collections for the period ending {DATE} total {AMOUNT}. Year-to-date collections are {AMOUNT}, which is {NUM}% of the annual budget projection. Accounts in delinquency: {NUM} (down from {NUM} last month). Three large commercial accounts are on formal payment plans totaling {AMOUNT} in scheduled receipts over the next {DAYS} days. No bond covenant concerns at this time. Recommend no rate action before the {DATE} council session.

subject_tmpl: Request: Write-Off Authorization for Uncollectible Accounts
body_tmpl: David, I've identified {NUM} accounts that have been delinquent for more than {DAYS} days with no contact and no payment activity. Total balance across these accounts is {AMOUNT}. Certified mail was returned undeliverable on all of them. Per our collections policy, these are eligible for write-off. I need your authorization to move them to the uncollectible ledger. I can provide the full account list whenever you're ready.
reply_tmpl: Lorena, please send the list. I'll cross-check against the property tax rolls before I authorize — sometimes we can pursue a lien instead of a write-off. Give me until {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Main Break on {STREET} — Billing Adjustment Needed for Affected Accounts
body_tmpl: Lorena, we had a water main break on {STREET} near {CROSS} on {DATE}. Repairs were completed by {TIME}. During that window some meters may have logged erroneous reads due to pressure fluctuations. I'm attaching the field report with affected addresses. Recommend we flag those accounts for a manual billing review before the next cycle goes out. Let me know what you need from my end.
reply_tmpl: Thanks Frank. I'll pull those addresses and put a review hold on the accounts. If any of them were already billed, I'll issue a credit. Can you send me the exact start/end times for the outage so I have it for the record?

body_tmpl: Yolanda, we received a FOIA request (case {CASE}) asking for billing records on account {ACCT} at {ADDRESS} from {DATE} through {DATE}. I can pull the records but want to confirm with you whether any information needs to be redacted before release. There may be personal financial data involved. Please let me know how you'd like to handle this and what the response deadline is.
reply_tmpl: Lorena — the deadline is {DAYS} business days from {DATE}. Please provide unredacted records to my office and I'll handle the review and any necessary redactions before release. Thanks.

subject_tmpl: Meter Replacement Program — Account Update Through {DATE}
body_tmpl: David, here's the status update on the meter replacement program through {DATE}. Of {NUM} meters scheduled for this phase, {NUM} have been replaced. {NUM} appointments are still pending, most due to access issues. We've had {NUM} refused entries requiring a second attempt. Average meter age on replacements so far: {AGE} years. Billing accuracy on replaced meters is tracking notably better — I'll have the before/after comparison ready for your budget presentation.

subject_tmpl: Meter Replacement Program — Capital Cost Estimate for Budget Amendment
body_tmpl: David, I've finalized the capital cost estimate for Phase {NUM} of the meter replacement program. Estimated cost is {AMOUNT} for {NUM} meters, including hardware, installation labor, and AMI infrastructure. I'd recommend we pursue the IEPA PWSLP grant to offset a portion — deadline is {DATE}. Can we set up time before the {DATE} council meeting to walk through the funding options? I want to make sure the Water Fund reserve is protected.
reply_tmpl: Tom, I'm available {DATE} at {TIME}. I'll pull the current fund balance and debt service schedule so we can look at how much we want to finance vs. fund from reserves. See you then.

subject_tmpl: Senior Discount Program — Eligibility List for Next Cycle
body_tmpl: Lorena, I'm attaching the updated eligibility list for the senior water discount program. {NUM} households qualify this cycle based on income verification completed through {DATE}. Please apply the {AMOUNT} monthly credit to their accounts before the next billing run. If any account on the list has a shutoff order pending, put those on hold pending my review — I want to make sure we're not cutting service to enrolled seniors.
reply_tmpl: Will do, David. I'll apply the credits today and flag any with pending shutoff orders for your review before we run field orders.

body_tmpl: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I received a shutoff notice saying my water will be disconnected on {DATE} unless I pay {AMOUNT}. I have been trying to set up a payment plan for weeks and keep getting the runaround. I have children in the house and losing water would be a health emergency. My address is {ADDRESS} and my account number is {ACCT}. I need someone to actually help me before the shutoff date. Please respond as soon as possible.
reply_tmpl: I'm so sorry for the stress this has caused. I've asked our Water Billing Clerk to contact you directly at your address on file to set up a payment arrangement right away. Your shutoff has been placed on administrative hold while we work this out. Please expect a call within {DAYS} business days.

body_tmpl: David, we had {NUM} returned payments in the {DATE} billing cycle totaling {AMOUNT}. {NUM} were returned checks (NSF) and {NUM} were failed ACH transactions. Per policy, I've assessed the {AMOUNT} returned payment fee on each account and sent notice letters. I'll be moving these back into delinquent status unless I hear otherwise. Do you want me to hold any of them for a direct outreach attempt before we escalate?

Mayor Okonkwo, I'm the owner of {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}. Our most recent water bill was {AMOUNT}, which is nearly double our normal monthly charge. We have not changed our operations or usage. We suspect a meter malfunction or billing error. I've called the water office three times and cannot get a resolution. Account number is {ACCT}. As a business that pays significant taxes to the Village, I expect this to be addressed promptly. Thank you.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for reaching out. I've asked our Water Billing Clerk and Public Works Director to jointly inspect the meter at {ADDRESS} and review the account within {DAYS} business days. We'll be in touch directly to report the findings and discuss any appropriate adjustment.

subject_tmpl: Water Rate Study — Timeline and Scope Confirmation
body_tmpl: David, I want to confirm scope and timeline for the water rate study before we issue the RFP. My understanding: the study should cover a {NUM}-year rate projection, include capital improvement needs from Tom's program, and factor in the bond reserve requirement. Target is to have a draft ready for the {DATE} committee meeting. Does this align with what you and Lorena have discussed? Let me know if there are additional data inputs we need to include.
reply_tmpl: Karen, that scope works for me. I'd also recommend including a tiered rate analysis — it came up at the last budget session. I can have preliminary data ready for the consultant by {DATE}. Let's loop in Tom and Lorena once the RFP is drafted.

body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, please find attached the quarterly delinquency report as of {DATE}. Total delinquent balance: {AMOUNT} across {NUM} accounts. This represents {NUM}% of total receivables, which is within the historical range. Residential delinquencies account for {AMOUNT}; commercial accounts make up the remainder. We have {NUM} accounts currently on active payment plans. I'll present a summary at the next department heads meeting if that's helpful.

Lorena, meter has been set and service activated at {ADDRESS} as of {DATE} at {TIME}. Meter serial number is {NUM}. Opening read is zero. This is a new residential connection. The permit was pulled under {LAST}. Please create the account and begin billing from today's date. I'll forward the inspection sign-off separately for your file.
reply_tmpl: Got it, Frank. Account created under {LAST} at {ADDRESS}, effective {DATE}. First bill will go out next cycle. Thanks for the quick turnaround on the meter set.

subject_tmpl: Meter Access Issue — {ADDRESS} — Second Attempt Needed
body_tmpl: Frank, meter reader was unable to access the meter at {ADDRESS} on {DATE}. The meter vault is blocked by a vehicle parked in the driveway. We left a door hanger asking the resident to call {PHONE} to reschedule. We'll attempt again on {DATE}. If access is denied a second time, I'll issue an estimated bill and flag the account. Heads up in case there's a crew already scheduled in that area.

Mayor Okonkwo, based on the completed rate study and projected capital needs, Finance is recommending a {NUM}% water rate increase effective {DATE}. The increase would generate an additional {AMOUNT} annually and fully fund the Year 1 meter replacement program without drawing down reserves below the minimum threshold. The average residential customer impact would be approximately {AMOUNT} per month. I'd like to present this formally at the {DATE} committee meeting. Please let me know if you'd like a pre-meeting briefing.
reply_tmpl: David, let's schedule a pre-meeting briefing for {DATE}. I want to understand the impact on low-income households before we go public. Can you also have Lorena pull the senior discount program numbers so we can discuss an expansion as part of the package?

body_tmpl: Lorena, I have a pending FOIA request (case {CASE}) seeking water billing records for property at {ADDRESS} for the period {DATE} to {DATE}. The requester is an attorney representing {LAST}. Response is due by {DATE}. Please pull the billing history, meter reads, and any adjustment records for that account and send to me no later than {DATE} so I have time to review before the deadline. Thank you.
reply_tmpl: Yolanda, records pulled and sent to your inbox now. I included billing history, two estimated bill periods, and the adjustment issued in {DATE}. Let me know if anything else is needed.

body_tmpl: David, I've negotiated a payment plan with {FIRST} {LAST} on account {ACCT}. Total past-due balance is {AMOUNT}. Terms: {AMOUNT} down payment received today, {AMOUNT} monthly payments over {NUM} months, and current billing to remain current throughout. I've documented the agreement and sent a copy to the account holder. Please make a note in your records and flag this account so it doesn't come up on the shutoff list during the repayment period.
reply_tmpl: Noted. I'll add the flag in the finance system. If they miss a payment, let me know right away so we can decide whether to reinstate the shutoff order.

subject_tmpl: Irrigation Meter Install — {ADDRESS} — Billing Change Needed
body_tmpl: Lorena, the irrigation meter installation at {ADDRESS} was completed on {DATE}. The property will now have two meters: the existing domestic meter {ACCT} and the new irrigation meter {ACCT}. The irrigation meter is exempt from the sewer surcharge. Please update the account structure accordingly before the {DATE} billing cycle. Let me know if you need the permit number or inspection sign-off to process the change.
reply_tmpl: Tom, I've split the account. Irrigation meter {ACCT} is set up as sewer-exempt beginning {DATE}. The owner should see two line items starting next cycle.

body_tmpl: David, I have a waiver request from {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS} (account {ACCT}). They're asking us to waive {AMOUNT} in interest charges that accrued during a period when they were deployed overseas with the military. They've provided documentation. Our ordinance allows waivers in hardship cases with Finance Director approval. I'd support granting this one. Please advise.
reply_tmpl: Lorena — approved. Please apply the credit and send the resident a written confirmation. Thank you for flagging this appropriately.

subject_tmpl: Suspected Vacant Property — Water Still Active — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Sandra, I want to flag {ADDRESS} to your attention. The property has had zero water consumption for {DAYS} days but the account remains active and billed at the minimum charge. That sometimes signals a vacant or abandoned property. Account number is {ACCT}. Could you do a drive-by or exterior check when you're in the area? If the property is confirmed vacant, we may want to discuss shutting off service. Let me know what you find.
reply_tmpl: Lorena, I'll swing by on {DATE}. I've had a separate complaint about that address anyway. Will report back.

subject_tmpl: Audit Finding — Billing Reconciliation Gap — Action Required
body_tmpl: Lorena, our external auditors flagged a reconciliation gap between water billing records and Finance receipts for the period {DATE} through {DATE}. The variance is {AMOUNT}. I need you to pull the billing summaries and payment posting reports for those months so we can trace the discrepancy. Please have them to me by {DATE}. This is time-sensitive — auditors return on {DATE}.
reply_tmpl: David, I'm on it. I'll pull the reports today and cross-reference the payment postings. My initial hunch is this may relate to the system migration in {DATE} — there were some posting delays. I'll document what I find.

subject_tmpl: Billing Error After Meter Replacement — Account {ACCT}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I had my water meter replaced on {DATE} and since then my bills have doubled. Before the new meter my bill was around {AMOUNT}. Now it's {AMOUNT}. I'm the only person in my home and nothing has changed. My address is {ADDRESS} and account number is {ACCT}. I believe the new meter is reading too high or was set up incorrectly. Can someone look into this? I'd really appreciate your help.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for bringing this to our attention. I've asked Public Works and our Water Billing Clerk to review the new meter installation and your account history. We want to make sure the replacement was done correctly and that your bills are accurate. Expect a follow-up within {DAYS} business days.

body_tmpl: Lorena, the structure at {ADDRESS} is scheduled for demolition on {DATE}. Per the permit, the water service must be terminated at the main before work begins. My crew will cut and cap the service on {DATE} at approximately {TIME}. Please close the water account effective that date and issue a final bill against {ACCT}. Coordinate with the building official if there are any outstanding permits tied to the address.
reply_tmpl: Thanks Frank. I'll close {ACCT} effective {DATE} and generate the final bill. I'll also shoot a note to Rosa so she knows the service has been terminated.

body_tmpl: David, here is the prepaid water account balance report as of {DATE}. We have {NUM} accounts on the prepayment plan. Total credits held: {AMOUNT}. Largest single credit balance is {AMOUNT} on account {ACCT}. Three accounts with credits over {AMOUNT} should probably be reviewed to see if the holder wants a refund or wants to keep the balance on account. I'll let you decide how you'd like to handle the refund side of that.

subject_tmpl: Water System Capital Plan — Summary for Council Packet
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I've compiled the water system capital plan summary for the upcoming council packet. Key items: meter replacement Phase {NUM} ({AMOUNT}), {STREET} water main rehab ({AMOUNT}), AMI system software upgrade ({AMOUNT}). Total five-year capital need: {AMOUNT}. Finance recommends funding {AMOUNT} from Water Fund reserves and pursuing grant opportunities for the balance. Tom has reviewed the technical scope and concurs with the cost estimates. Let me know if you need any changes before it goes to packet.
reply_tmpl: Karen, looks solid. Please add a one-paragraph executive summary on the rate impact so council members have that context upfront before reading the full plan.

body_tmpl: David, I've received a hardship request from {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. Account {ACCT} has a past-due balance of {AMOUNT}. The resident provided documentation of a recent job loss. They are requesting either a waiver of late fees ({AMOUNT}) or an extended payment plan of {NUM} months. I don't have authority to approve either without your sign-off. Given the documentation, I'd recommend the fee waiver and a {NUM}-month plan. Please advise as soon as possible — shutoff was scheduled for {DATE}.
reply_tmpl: Lorena, approved on both — waive the fees and set up the {NUM}-month plan. Move the shutoff date to at least {DATE} to give them time to make the first payment. Send me a copy of the plan once it's signed.

body_tmpl: Officer Banks, we have a scheduled water shutoff at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} at approximately {TIME}. The account holder ({LAST}) has a history of refusing access and was verbally confrontational during the last visit. I'm requesting a police presence when our crew arrives, just as a precaution. Please let me know if this is something you can accommodate or if I should coordinate differently.
reply_tmpl: Lorena, I'll have a unit available. Please confirm the exact time the morning of {DATE} and we'll have someone there. Thank you for the heads-up.

body_tmpl: Lorena, we're approaching the fiscal year-end close. I need all water billing receivables posted no later than {DATE} to ensure they're captured in the correct fiscal year. That means any bills issued, credits applied, or adjustments made need to be finalized by {TIME} on {DATE}. If you have anything in process that might not make that deadline, flag it to me today. Also, please confirm the final delinquent balance figure so I can post it to the Water Fund balance sheet.
reply_tmpl: David, understood. I'll make sure everything is posted by {TIME} on {DATE}. I'll send you the confirmed delinquent balance by end of day today. One pending adjustment for account {ACCT} may need your approval to make the deadline — sending that over separately now.

subject_tmpl: AMI System — Failed Read Report — {DATE} Cycle
body_tmpl: David, the AMI system generated {NUM} failed reads this billing cycle ending {DATE}. Of those, {NUM} are likely radio frequency interference (flagged by the vendor), and {NUM} appear to be meter hardware faults. I'm estimating {NUM} accounts this cycle, and I want to flag the failed-read total before we close since it affects revenue recognition. Vendor is scheduled for a site visit on {DATE}. I'll send an updated report once the outstanding reads are resolved.
